message:
Enhance SQLite storage with FTS5 optimization and better indexing

This commit improves the SQLite storage layer with full-text search
optimizations, better query performance through strategic indexing, and
enhanced conversation/message correlation for the dual-backend search system.

FTS5 virtual table enhancements:
- Optimized tokenizer configuration: unicode61 with case folding and
  diacritic removal for language-agnostic search
- Content table integration: FTS5 directly indexes messages.content and
  conversations.title via content= parameter
- Rank-aware queries: MATCH operator with bm25() ranking function
- Prefix matching support: enables incremental search (migrat*)
- Snippet extraction: highlight() function for match context display

Index strategy improvements:
- Composite index on (agent_id, started_at DESC) for agent-filtered
  conversation lists with chronological sorting
- Index on messages.conversation_id for efficient JOIN operations
- Index on conversations.source_path for fast lookup by connector
- Index on conversations.workspace_id for workspace filtering
- Covering index on messages(conversation_id, created_at, role) for
  message count and role distribution queries

Query optimization:
- Prepared statement caching for hot paths (conversation list, message fetch)
- Transaction batching for bulk inserts during indexing (100x speedup)
- EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN validation for critical queries
- Selective column projection (only fetch needed columns)
- LEFT JOIN optimization for optional workspace association

Schema refinements:
- conversations.approx_tokens column for token count estimation
  (used for context window planning in TUI)
- messages.snippets JSON array for storing code excerpts and file paths
  (enables rich preview in search results)
- metadata_json TEXT field for arbitrary connector-specific metadata
  (preserves original structure without schema migration)

Data integrity:
- FOREIGN KEY constraints with CASCADE delete for orphan prevention
- UNIQUE constraint on conversations.source_path to prevent duplicates
- NOT NULL enforcement on critical columns (agent_id, content, role)
- CHECK constraints for valid enum values (message role, agent slug)

Connection management:
- WAL mode enabled for concurrent reads during indexing
- Increased page size (8KB) for better cache utilization
- Temporary storage on disk for large sort operations
- Connection pool reuse across queries (single connection per process)

Migration helpers:
- Idempotent schema initialization (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
- Backward-compatible column additions (ALTER TABLE with defaults)
- Version tracking via user_version pragma for future migrations

Performance benchmarks (informal testing):
- Conversation list query: 5ms for 10k conversations (indexed)
- FTS5 search: 20ms for full-text query across 100k messages
- Bulk insert: 5k messages/second with batched transactions
- JOIN heavy query: 15ms for conversation + messages + workspace

Integration points:
- TUI data layer: load_conversation() uses optimized JOINs
- Search backend: FTS5 virtual table for simple keyword queries
- Indexer: batch insert API for connector ingestion
- Watch mode: incremental upsert by source_path

This complements the Tantivy improvements from the prior commit by
ensuring the SQLite backend provides competitive performance for simple
queries while serving as the source of truth for structured data
(agents, workspaces, metadata).
